basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Ḥajj — The Pilgrimage — opens on the convulsion of the Hour, that quaking in which every nursing mother forgets her infant and every pregnant woman delivers her burden, in which people appear drunk without being drunk. Qushayrī discerns there two intoxications: the drunkenness of wine, proper to the people of heedlessness, and the drunkenness of love, proper to the people of union. The sūra unfolds the proof of the Resurrection through the stages of creation and the earth that water revives, then proclaims Abraham's call to the Pilgrimage: people come “from every deep ravine,” on foot or on lean mounts. It ordains the rites, the circling of the ancient House, the offerings — and culminates on this decisive word: “Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches God, but the godfearing devotion from you (al-taqwā)”, for in Qushayrī's reading no worth lies in the materiality of the act, but in its conjunction with sincerity, and only that reaches God which contemplates Him in oneness, without a gaze cast upon others. Then comes the permission to fight granted to the wronged, the reminder that without God's repelling of people by one another, monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques would be demolished, the avowal that the idolaters “did not measure God His true measure.” It is sealed on the call to bow, prostrate, worship, and strive for God “a true striving — the jihād,” holding fast to Him alone: to hold fast to God is to disown all power of one's own and to rise to the worship of God by God for God — “He is your Protector, what an excellent Protector and what an excellent Helper!”
themes: the taqwā that alone reaches God
striving and holding fast to God

versets.ar: يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ السَّاعَةِ شَيْءٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu ttaqū rabbakum inna zalzalata s-sāʿati shayʾun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: O mankind! Fear your Lord! The convulsion of the Hour is indeed a tremendous thing!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two modes of address: “O mankind!” is a call of warning (nidāʾ ʿalāma), “O you who believe!” a call of honor (nidāʾ karāma); the Real opens His discourses with both, according as He warns or enlightens. Taqwā, he says, is to guard oneself, to take refuge, and to shun the forbidden: shunning what is forbidden is obligation, shunning the superfluous is supererogation. By “fear” He frightens them, then by “your Lord” He soothes their fear: for to hear of lordship calls forth perseverance and beautiful sufficiency.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.def: taqwā: to guard against the forbidden and the superfluous.

mots.racine: ز ل ز ل / س و ع

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَرَوْنَهَا تَذْهَلُ كُلُّ مُرْضِعَةٍ عَمَّا أَرْضَعَتْ وَتَضَعُ كُلُّ ذَاتِ حَمْلٍ حَمْلَهَا وَتَرَى النَّاسَ سُكَارَىٰ وَمَا هُم بِسُكَارَىٰ وَلَٰكِنَّ عَذَابَ اللَّهِ شَدِيدٌ
versets.tr: yawma tarawnahā tadhhalu kullu murḍiʿatin ʿammā arḍaʿat wa-taḍaʿu kullu dhāti ḥamlin ḥamlahā wa-tarā n-nāsa sukārā wa-mā hum bi-sukārā wa-lākinna ʿadhāba llāhi shadīdun
versets.trad: On the Day you behold it, every nursing mother will forget her infant and every pregnant woman will deliver her burden. You will see people as if drunk, though they are not drunk; but the chastisement of God is severe!
versets.commentaire: The terror of that Day absorbs and engulfs all. Qushayrī pauses on the intoxication: they all resemble one another in seeming drunk, but the cause differs — in one the drunkenness springs from the terror that seizes him, in another from his annihilation in the very source of union. Thus he marks out two drunkennesses: the drunkenness of wine, which is that of the people of heedlessness, and the drunkenness of love, which is that of the people of union — and how far apart the two!

mots.racine: ذ ه ل / ر ض ع
mots.def: dhahala: to be distracted, to forget in shock.

mots.racine: س ك ر

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يُجَادِلُ فِي اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَيَتَّبِعُ كُلَّ شَيْطَانٍ مَّرِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yujādilu fī llāhi bi-ghayri ʿilmin wa-yattabiʿu kulla shayṭānin marīdin
versets.trad: Among people is one who disputes about God without knowledge and follows every rebellious devil,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a clear line: to dispute for God, against the enemies of the Truth and the deniers, is among the things that bring one near to Him; but to dispute about God, to wrangle against His friends, and to persist in falsehood after the proofs have shone, are the marks of wretchedness. What proceeds from the devil's whisperings and goadings has no end but the Fire.

mots.racine: ج د ل
ش ط ن / م ر د

versets.ar: كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّهُ مَن تَوَلَّاهُ فَأَنَّهُ يُضِلُّهُ وَيَهْدِيهِ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ
versets.tr: kutiba ʿalayhi annahu man tawallāhu fa-annahu yuḍilluhu wa-yahdīhi ilā ʿadhābi s-saʿīri
versets.trad: of whom it is decreed that whoever takes him as ally, he leads him astray and guides him to the chastisement of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Whoever aligns himself with the devil by following his calls, the devil guides him only to error. Qushayrī adds that in the hereafter the devil will disown those who followed him and curse the whole company of his adherents. Hence his prayer: “We seek refuge in God from the devil and his promptings, from the abyss of wretchedness and the evil of his sudden assaults.”

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.def: tawallā: to take as patron, to side with.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / س ع ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِن كُنتُمْ فِي رَيْبٍ مِّنَ الْبَعْثِ فَإِنَّا خَلَقْنَاكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ مِن مُّضْغَةٍ مُّخَلَّقَةٍ وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ لِّنُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ ۚ وَنُقِرُّ فِي الْأَرْحَامِ مَا نَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ثُمَّ نُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلًا ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوا أَشُدَّكُمْ ۖ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ لِكَيْلَا يَعْلَمَ مِن بَعْدِ عِلْمٍ شَيْئًا ۚ وَتَرَى الْأَرْضَ هَامِدَةً فَإِذَا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا الْمَاءَ اهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ وَأَنبَتَتْ مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍ بَهِيجٍ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu in kuntum fī raybin mina l-baʿthi fa-innā khalaqnākum min turābin thumma min nuṭfatin thumma min ʿalaqatin thumma min muḍghatin mukhallaqatin wa-ghayri mukhallaqatin li-nubayyina lakum wa-nuqirru fī l-arḥāmi mā nashāʾu ilā ajalin musamman thumma nukhrijukum ṭiflan thumma li-tablughū ashuddakum wa-minkum man yutawaffā wa-minkum man yuraddu ilā ardhali l-ʿumuri li-kaylā yaʿlama min baʿdi ʿilmin shayʾan wa-tarā l-arḍa hāmidatan fa-idhā anzalnā ʿalayhā l-māʾa htazzat wa-rabat wa-anbatat min kulli zawjin bahījin
versets.trad: O mankind! If you are in doubt concerning the Resurrection, then know that We created you from dust, then from a drop of fluid, then from a clinging clot, then from a lump of flesh, formed and unformed, that We may make clear to you Our power. And We settle in the wombs what We will for an appointed term; then We bring you forth as infants, that you may then reach your full strength. Some of you are taken in death, and some are driven back to the most abject age, so that he knows nothing after having known. And you see the earth lifeless, but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells and brings forth every kind of lovely growth.
versets.commentaire: God argues the resurrection from what they already conceded: that He created them and brought them from state to state — from drop to clot, from youth to old age, then to death; and from the earth He revives in spring after its death. He who can do all this can bring life out of decayed bones. On “the most abject age,” Qushayrī unfolds a spiritual range: it is the time of slackening after striving, the state of veiling after contemplation, the pursuit of personal shares after fulfilling the rights, the return to the dwellings of disobedience, the surrender to the soul's governance and blindness to the contemplation of God's decree.

mots.racine: ر ي ب / ب ع ث
ن ط ف
mots.def: nuṭfa: a drop of seminal fluid.

mots.racine: ر ذ ل / ع م ر
ه ز ز / ر ب و

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ وَأَنَّهُ يُحْيِي الْمَوْتَىٰ وَأَنَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha huwa l-ḥaqqu wa-annahu yuḥyi l-mawtā wa-annahu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: That is because God is the Truth, that He gives life to the dead, and that He has power over all things,
versets.commentaire: “God is the Real” — al-Ḥaqq, says Qushayrī, the Real in Himself, the possessor of the real. And “He gives life to the dead” is not only the spring earth: He gives life to souls through the grace of worship, and to hearts through the lights of contemplations; He revives the states of seekers by the beauty of His turning toward them. The life of the moments, he adds, depends on conformity to the command, then on beautiful acceptance and the stillness of the soul under the flow of the decree.

mots.racine: ح ق ق
ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gives life to the dead
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life, to resurrect.

versets.ar: وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهَا وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَبْعَثُ مَن فِي الْقُبُورِ
versets.tr: wa-anna s-sāʿata ātiyatun lā rayba fīhā wa-anna llāha yabʿathu man fī l-qubūri
versets.trad: and because the Hour is coming, no doubt of it, and because God will raise up those who are in the graves.
versets.commentaire: The Hour comes without doubt, and God will raise those in the graves. People's denial stems from measuring God's power by the yardstick of their own incapacity, whereas He who revives the dead earth all the more revives dissolved bodies. Faith in the Hour is the bearing that orders the servant's whole relation to his end.

mots.racine: س و ع / أ ت ي
ب ع ث / ق ب ر
mots.sens: He raises those in the graves

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يُجَادِلُ فِي اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَلَا هُدًى وَلَا كِتَابٍ مُّنِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yujādilu fī llāhi bi-ghayri ʿilmin wa-lā hudan wa-lā kitābin munīrin
versets.trad: And among people is one who disputes about God without knowledge, without guidance, and without an illuminating Book.
versets.commentaire: The implied sense, observes Qushayrī, permits dispute about God when the disputant possesses the knowledge of proof and argument, that he may defend his religion — for God says to His Prophet: “Dispute with them in the best manner.” Whoever does not master the adversary's doctrine cannot extricate himself from it; hence he infers the obligation of learning the science of foundations (uṣūl), a refutation of those who disparage it.

mots.racine: غ ي ر / ع ل م
ك ت ب / ن و ر

versets.ar: ثَانِيَ عِطْفِهِ لِيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۖ لَهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا خِزْيٌ ۖ وَنُذِيقُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَذَابَ الْحَرِيقِ
versets.tr: thāniya ʿiṭfihi li-yuḍilla ʿan sabīli llāhi lahu fī d-dunyā khizyun wa-nudhīquhu yawma l-qiyāmati ʿadhāba l-ḥarīqi
versets.trad: turning his side in scorn, to lead astray from the path of God. For him is disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the chastisement of the burning Fire:
versets.commentaire: “Turning his side in scorn”: Qushayrī sees in this the arrogant one who disdains to receive the Truth, indifferent to acquiring it, not placing his gaze where it belongs — for did he do so, he would readily free himself of his doubts. For such a man: in this world disgrace and humiliation, in the next the chastisement of fire. The blindness is not in the thing but in the willful turning away.

mots.racine: ث ن ي / ع ط ف
mots.def: thanā ʿiṭfahu: to turn away in disdain.

mots.racine: خ ز ي

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاكَ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ
versets.tr: dhālika bimā qaddamat yadāka wa-anna llāha laysa bi-ẓallāmin li-l-ʿabīdi
versets.trad: “This is for what your hands have sent before, for God is in no way unjust to His servants!”
versets.commentaire: “This is for what your hands have sent before”: the chastisement is but the fruit of one's own act. Qushayrī recalls that God is in no way unjust to His servants: none receives but according to what he has done, and the decree remains pure equity. Man is returned to his own deed, with no grievance to lay against his Lord.

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.sens: for what your hands sent before
mots.def: qaddama: to send ahead, to do.

mots.racine: ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ حَرْفٍ ۖ فَإِنْ أَصَابَهُ خَيْرٌ اطْمَأَنَّ بِهِ ۖ وَإِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ فِتْنَةٌ انقَلَبَ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهِ خَسِرَ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْخُسْرَانُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yaʿbudu llāha ʿalā ḥarfin fa-in aṣābahu khayrun iṭmaʾanna bihi wa-in aṣābathu fitnatun nqalaba ʿalā wajhihi khasira d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirata dhālika huwa l-khusrānu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: And among people is one who worships God on an edge: if good befalls him, he is content with it; but if a trial befalls him, he turns about on his face, losing both this world and the hereafter. That is the manifest loss!
versets.commentaire: “To worship God on an edge”: Qushayrī describes one who stands at the margin, without sincerity — neither an assent that yields accord, nor a denial that marks rupture. Good and ease reassure him; but let a trial strike him, and he turns back on his heels, belying by his act the accord he had displayed. Such is the man who has lost in both abodes and failed at both stations.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ح ر ف
ق ل ب / و ج ه
mots.sens: he turns about on his face

versets.ar: يَدْعُو مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَضُرُّهُ وَمَا لَا يَنفَعُهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الضَّلَالُ الْبَعِيدُ
versets.tr: yadʿū min dūni llāhi mā lā yaḍurruhu wa-mā lā yanfaʿuhu dhālika huwa ḍ-ḍalālu l-baʿīdu
versets.trad: He calls, besides God, upon that which neither harms him nor profits him: that is the far-gone error!
versets.commentaire: He worships that whose harm, in the very worship, outweighs any profit — rather, there is in this worship no profit at all. The certain harm of idol-worship, says Qushayrī, lays bare the feebleness of their minds and shows people the error of their deed; and the profit they imagine has neither attainment nor reality.

mots.racine: ض ر ر
ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: يَدْعُو لَمَن ضَرُّهُ أَقْرَبُ مِن نَّفْعِهِ ۚ لَبِئْسَ الْمَوْلَىٰ وَلَبِئْسَ الْعَشِيرُ
versets.tr: yadʿū la-man ḍarruhu aqrabu min nafʿihi la-biʾsa l-mawlā wa-la-biʾsa l-ʿashīru
versets.trad: He calls upon one whose harm is nearer than his benefit. What an evil patron and what an evil companion!
versets.commentaire: “What an evil patron, what an evil companion!” Qushayrī comments: what an evil helper is the idol for them, and what an evil people are they for the idol — and how should they not be, since for its sake they fell into perpetual chastisement? Alliance with what has neither power nor knowledge leads only to shared ruin.

mots.racine: ض ر ر / ق ر ب
ب أ س / و ل ي

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ
versets.tr: inna llāha yudkhilu lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru inna llāha yafʿalu mā yurīdu
versets.trad: Truly God admits those who believe and do righteous deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. Truly God does what He wills!
versets.commentaire: “Those who believed,” says Qushayrī, are those who attested and then realized: faith has attestation as its outward, realization as its inward, and none attains both save by grace. The good deed is that which is fit for acceptance and reward. The Gardens are of two kinds: the deferred — reward and repentance; and the immediate — states and nearness, after the verse, “for him who fears the station of his Lord, two Gardens.”

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: āmana: to believe, attest and realize.

mots.racine: ج ر ي / ن ه ر

versets.ar: مَن كَانَ يَظُنُّ أَن لَّن يَنصُرَهُ اللَّهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ بِسَبَبٍ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ ثُمَّ لْيَقْطَعْ فَلْيَنظُرْ هَلْ يُذْهِبَنَّ كَيْدُهُ مَا يَغِيظُ
versets.tr: man kāna yaẓunnu an lan yanṣurahu llāhu fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati fa-l-yamdud bi-sababin ilā s-samāʾi thumma l-yaqṭaʿ fa-l-yanẓur hal yudhhibanna kayduhu mā yaghīẓu
versets.trad: Whoever thinks that God will not help him in this world and the hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the sky, then let him cut it, and let him see whether his scheme can remove what enrages him!
versets.commentaire: The Real, says Qushayrī, crushes the enemies of His Messenger; so whoever takes no joy in seeing God reserve His singling-out for whom He wills, let him go strangle himself in rage — and it will avail him nothing, as was said: “If you cannot accept what you see, here is the rope: hang yourself with it.” Impotent fury changes nothing of the decree.

mots.racine: م د د / س ب ب
غ ي ظ

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَاهُ آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَن يُرِيدُ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika anzalnāhu āyātin bayyinātin wa-anna llāha yahdī man yurīdu
versets.trad: Thus have We sent it down as clear signs, and God guides whom He wills.
versets.commentaire: “Clear signs”: Qushayrī divides them into signs posited by reason, signs posited by transmission, and warning-signs given at the moments of spiritual dealings — what the servant experiences of constriction, seizure, loss, or trial when he has failed a duty; or of expansion, the sweetness of obedience, and facilitation. Then come the signs in the secrets, which are the Real's intimate discourse; and he concludes: “the signs are manifest, the proofs resplendent, but all hinges on the one who knows how to see.”

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ب ي ن
ه د ي / ر و د

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هَادُوا وَالصَّابِئِينَ وَالنَّصَارَىٰ وَالْمَجُوسَ وَالَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-lladhīna hādū wa-ṣ-ṣābiʾīna wa-n-naṣārā wa-l-majūsa wa-lladhīna ashrakū inna llāha yafṣilu baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdun
versets.trad: Truly the believers, the Jews, the Sabeans, the Christians, the Magians, and those who associate partners — God will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Truly God is witness over all things.
versets.commentaire: All categories of humankind, says Qushayrī, despite the diversity of their ranks — the friend and the enemy, the affirmer of Unity and its denier — will be gathered on the Day of Gathering; then the Real will deal with each according to what He promised: either union without end, or states without limit. The moment is one, yet each comes to what was prepared for him, and to that for which he was created.

mots.racine: ص ب أ
mots.def: a religious group named in the Qurʾān.

mots.racine: ف ص ل

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَسْجُدُ لَهُ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَن فِي الْأَرْضِ وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ وَالنُّجُومُ وَالْجِبَالُ وَالشَّجَرُ وَالدَّوَابُّ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنَ النَّاسِ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ الْعَذَابُ ۗ وَمَن يُهِنِ اللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُ مِن مُّكْرِمٍ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يَشَاءُ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha yasjudu lahu man fī s-samāwāti wa-man fī l-arḍi wa-sh-shamsu wa-l-qamaru wa-n-nujūmu wa-l-jibālu wa-sh-shajaru wa-d-dawābbu wa-kathīrun mina n-nāsi wa-kathīrun ḥaqqa ʿalayhi l-ʿadhābu wa-man yuhini llāhu fa-mā lahu min mukrimin inna llāha yafʿalu mā yashāʾu
versets.trad: Do you not see that to God prostrate all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth — the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, the beasts, and many among mankind? Yet many deserve the chastisement. Whomever God humiliates, none can honor. Truly God does what He wills.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two prostrations: the people of gnosis prostrate with a prostration of worship; the deniers, every particle of them prostrates with a prostration of indication and witness — after the verse: “In everything He has a sign attesting that He is One.” Thus the whole creation, willingly or unwillingly, proclaims the Oneness of its Creator.

mots.racine: س ج د
ه و ن

versets.ar: هَٰذَانِ خَصْمَانِ اخْتَصَمُوا فِي رَبِّهِمْ ۖ فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا قُطِّعَتْ لَهُمْ ثِيَابٌ مِّن نَّارٍ يُصَبُّ مِن فَوْقِ رُؤُوسِهِمُ الْحَمِيمُ
versets.tr: hādhāni khaṣmāni khtaṣamū fī rabbihim fa-lladhīna kafarū quṭṭiʿat lahum thiyābun min nārin yuṣabbu min fawqi ruʾūsihimu l-ḥamīmu
versets.trad: These are two adversaries who dispute concerning their Lord. As for those who disbelieve, garments of fire are cut out for them, and over their heads is poured boiling water,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the garments into a spiritual parable. The disbelievers today wear the garment of association, embroidered with deprivation; and tomorrow the pitch, embroidered with abandonment. But the believers today wear taqwā as a garment — flight from association, then from transgression, then from heedlessness, then from rest in other than God; and tomorrow silk, or the garment of love, or that of solitude with Him — the highest being the people of stripping-bare (tajrīd), freed from the bondage of all that becoming has touched.

mots.racine: خ ص م
ق ط ع / ث و ب / ن و ر
mots.sens: garments of fire are cut out

versets.ar: يُصْهَرُ بِهِ مَا فِي بُطُونِهِمْ وَالْجُلُودُ
versets.tr: yuṣharu bihi mā fī buṭūnihim wa-l-julūdu
versets.trad: melting what is in their bellies, and their skins.
versets.commentaire: The boiling water melts what is in the bellies and the skins. The bodily torment here is, for Qushayrī, but the sensory counterpart of a spiritual estrangement: the garment of abandonment (khidhlān) that veils the heart already in this world. Outer severity answers the inner rupture from the Real.

mots.racine: ص ه ر
ب ط ن / ج ل د

versets.ar: وَلَهُم مَّقَامِعُ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-lahum maqāmiʿu min ḥadīdin
versets.trad: And for them are maces of iron.
versets.commentaire: “For them maces of iron”: to the hardness of their hearts answers the hardness of iron. The chastisement, in the allusive reading, prolongs the state of the hardened heart, deaf to the call: man receives in sensory form what his inner self was. The striking iron is the reverse of the hardness that refused the Truth.

mots.racine: ق م ع
ح د د

versets.ar: كُلَّمَا أَرَادُوا أَن يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهَا مِنْ غَمٍّ أُعِيدُوا فِيهَا وَذُوقُوا عَذَابَ الْحَرِيقِ
versets.tr: kullamā arādū an yakhrujū minhā min ghammin uʿīdū fīhā wa-dhūqū ʿadhāba l-ḥarīqi
versets.trad: Whenever they wish, in their anguish, to come out of it, they are returned to it: “Taste the chastisement of the burning Fire!”
versets.commentaire: Whenever they wish to come out of the anguish, they are returned to it. Unlike the believer whom God brings out of darkness into light, the disbeliever is endlessly sent back to his anguish: no escape for the one who has turned away. “Taste!” is the word that seals the closure upon them, like the “Remain in it, despised, and speak not to Me” that Qushayrī cites.

mots.racine: ر و د / خ ر ج
mots.sens: whenever they wish to come out

mots.racine: ع و د

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ
versets.tr: inna llāha yudkhilu lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru yuḥallawna fīhā min asāwira min dhahabin wa-luʾluʾan wa-libāsuhum fīhā ḥarīrun
versets.trad: Truly God admits those who believe and do righteous deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. There they are adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garment there is silk.
versets.commentaire: The adornment, says Qushayrī, is a protection for them, a veil over their states: they are the ornament of the Garden, not the Garden their ornament. And he cites: “When the pearl adorns the beauty of faces, it is the beauty of your face that adorns the pearl.” The elect is not made beautiful by the place; rather it is he who makes its beauty.

mots.racine: ح ل ي
س و ر / ذ ه ب

versets.ar: وَهُدُوا إِلَى الطَّيِّبِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَهُدُوا إِلَىٰ صِرَاطِ الْحَمِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-hudū ilā ṭ-ṭayyibi mina l-qawli wa-hudū ilā ṣirāṭi l-ḥamīdi
versets.trad: They were guided to the good word, and guided to the path of the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: The good word, Qushayrī enumerates, is that which issues from a pure heart and a limpid secret, untainted by any disagreement with the foundations of Unity; it is also exhortation to seekers, the guiding of aspirants toward God, the enjoining of good, supplication for the believers, the word of truth before one feared or hoped for, the two testimonies from a sincere heart. And “the path of the Praised” is the approved road, the one the Law attests as sound, against which Reality finds nothing to object: the path of following (ittibāʿ) and not of innovation.

mots.racine: ط ي ب / ق و ل
ص ر ط / ح م د

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَالْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ الَّذِي جَعَلْنَاهُ لِلنَّاسِ سَوَاءً الْعَاكِفُ فِيهِ وَالْبَادِ ۚ وَمَن يُرِدْ فِيهِ بِإِلْحَادٍ بِظُلْمٍ نُّذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-yaṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi lladhī jaʿalnāhu li-n-nāsi sawāʾan il-ʿākifu fīhi wa-l-bādi wa-man yurid fīhi bi-ilḥādin bi-ẓulmin nudhiqhu min ʿadhābin alīmin
versets.trad: As for those who disbelieve and bar others from the path of God and from the Sacred Mosque, which We made for mankind — equal in it are the resident and the visitor — and whoever seeks therein to profane it through wrongdoing, We shall make him taste a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Barring from the Sacred Mosque is done, says Qushayrī, by terrorizing the roads, by seizing the wealth which, left with its owner, would have brought him to the House. On “equal, the resident and the visitor,” he unfolds a mystical image: at the meeting-place of the noble, access is equal for all; whoever reaches this courtyard suffers neither rank nor rejection — after arrival, no barrier and no distance remain. On the road, advance and delay still count; but in the arrival, no difference remains.

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: they bar from the path of God

mots.racine: س و ي / ع ك ف / ب د و

versets.ar: وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ مَكَانَ الْبَيْتِ أَن لَّا تُشْرِكْ بِي شَيْئًا وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقَائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ
versets.tr: wa-idh bawwaʾnā li-ibrāhīma makāna l-bayti an lā tushrik bī shayʾan wa-ṭahhir baytiya li-ṭ-ṭāʾifīna wa-l-qāʾimīna wa-r-rukkaʿi s-sujūdi
versets.trad: And when We assigned to Abraham the site of the House: “Associate nothing with Me; and purify My House for those who circle it, those who stand, and those who bow and prostrate.”
versets.commentaire: In the sense of allusion, Qushayrī turns the command inward. “Associate nothing with Me”: regard neither the House nor your building of it. “Purify My House” — by the tongue of knowledge, it is the Kaʿba; by indication, it is: empty your heart of all things save the remembrance of God. He cites this divine saying: “Empty for Me a house that I may dwell in it” — “Which house?” — “the heart of My believing servant.” And purification has degrees: from heedlessness, then from attachment to all other than God, then from the gaze cast upon one's own deed, then from repose in the states themselves.

mots.racine: ب و أ
mots.def: bawwaʾa: to establish, settle a place.

mots.racine: ط ه ر / ب ي ت
mots.def: ṭahhara: to purify; the heart in allusion.

mots.racine: ر ك ع / س ج د

versets.ar: وَأَذِّن فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِّ يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالًا وَعَلَىٰ كُلِّ ضَامِرٍ يَأْتِينَ مِن كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ
versets.tr: wa-adhdhin fī n-nāsi bi-l-ḥajji yaʾtūka rijālan wa-ʿalā kulli ḍāmirin yaʾtīna min kulli fajjin ʿamīqin
versets.trad: And proclaim to mankind the Pilgrimage: they will come to you on foot and on every lean mount, coming from every deep ravine,
versets.commentaire: Abraham proclaimed, and God made his call heard by all the progeny in the loins of their fathers; then responded whoever, in God's knowledge, was destined to make the pilgrimage. Qushayrī notes that those on foot are named before the mounts because their effort is greater. And “coming from every deep ravine” is said, he observes, by way of praise and gratitude: God magnifies their act and exalts their coming.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن / ح ج ج
ض م ر
ف ج ج / ع م ق
mots.def: fajj: mountain pass; ʿamīq: deep, far.

versets.ar: لِّيَشْهَدُوا مَنَافِعَ لَهُمْ وَيَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَّعْلُومَاتٍ عَلَىٰ مَا رَزَقَهُم مِّن بَهِيمَةِ الْأَنْعَامِ ۖ فَكُلُوا مِنْهَا وَأَطْعِمُوا الْبَائِسَ الْفَقِيرَ
versets.tr: li-yashhadū manāfiʿa lahum wa-yadhkurū sma llāhi fī ayyāmin maʿlūmātin ʿalā mā razaqahum min bahīmati l-anʿāmi fa-kulū minhā wa-aṭʿimū l-bāʾisa l-faqīra
versets.trad: that they may witness benefits for themselves and mention the name of God, on appointed days, over the grazing beasts He has provided them. So eat of them and feed the wretched and the poor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades the “benefits” by rank: for the wealthy, their goods; for the people of works, the sweetness of their obedience; for the people of states, the limpidity of their breaths; and for the people of Unity, their contentment with the choice the Real brings forth for them from the Unseen. As for mentioning the Name over the offerings, some do so while slaughtering their own desires and choices with the knives of renunciation, until they stand by God, for God, effacing all that is not God. Sharing with the poor draws down the blessing of the poor.

mots.racine: ش ه د / ن ف ع
ب أ س / ف ق ر

versets.ar: ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ وَلْيَطَّوَّفُوا بِالْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيقِ
versets.tr: thumma l-yaqḍū tafathahum wa-l-yūfū nudhūrahum wa-l-yaṭṭawwafū bi-l-bayti l-ʿatīqi
versets.trad: Then let them complete their rites, fulfill their vows, and circle the ancient House.
versets.commentaire: “Let them fulfill their vows”: Qushayrī carries this to the inner pact. He whose vow was repentance, his fulfilling is not to return to disobedience; he whose pact was to embrace obedience, not to fall short of it; he whose pact was to seek neither station nor honor, his fidelity is the uprightness that demands neither share nor gain. And the circumambulation (ṭawāf) is threefold: by the body around the House, by the heart in the kingdom of the heavens, by the secret in the expanses of the Malakūt.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / ت ف ث
ب ي ت / ع ت ق

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ وَمَن يُعَظِّمْ حُرُمَاتِ اللَّهِ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُ عِندَ رَبِّهِ ۗ وَأُحِلَّتْ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعَامُ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَاجْتَنِبُوا الرِّجْسَ مِنَ الْأَوْثَانِ وَاجْتَنِبُوا قَوْلَ الزُّورِ
versets.tr: dhālika wa-man yuʿaẓẓim ḥurumāti llāhi fa-huwa khayrun lahu ʿinda rabbihi wa-uḥillat lakumu l-anʿāmu illā mā yutlā ʿalaykum fa-jtanibū r-rijsa mina l-awthāni wa-jtanibū qawla z-zūri
versets.trad: Such it is. And whoever honors the sacred things of God, that is better for him with his Lord. The grazing beasts are made lawful for you, except those recited to you. So shun the filth of idols, and shun false speech,
versets.commentaire: “To magnify the sacred things of God is to magnify His command, and to magnify His command is not to disobey Him,” Qushayrī sums up. He warns gravely: every other transgression leaves a passage for pardon and a road for hope; but to violate the sacred is at the peril of not being forgiven, for it can ruin the religion and the very Unity of a man. As for “false speech,” it includes the word of the tongue unsupported by the word of the heart — and whoever pacts with God by his heart yet does not keep it falls under false speech.

mots.racine: ع ظ م / ح ر م
mots.sens: magnifies the sacred things of God

mots.racine: ق و ل / ز و ر

versets.ar: حُنَفَاءَ لِلَّهِ غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَكَأَنَّمَا خَرَّ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَتَخْطَفُهُ الطَّيْرُ أَوْ تَهْوِي بِهِ الرِّيحُ فِي مَكَانٍ سَحِيقٍ
versets.tr: ḥunafāʾa lillāhi ghayra mushrikīna bihi wa-man yushrik bi-llāhi fa-kaʾannamā kharra mina s-samāʾi fa-takhṭafuhu ṭ-ṭayru aw tahwī bihi r-rīḥu fī makānin saḥīqin
versets.trad: as pure monotheists devoted to God, associating nothing with Him. Whoever associates anything with God is as though he had fallen from the sky and the birds snatch him away, or the wind sweeps him to a far-off place.
versets.commentaire: The ḥanīf, says Qushayrī, is one who inclines to the true away from the false — in heart and soul, outwardly and in secret, in deeds, states, and words. And association, he adds, is of two kinds: manifest and hidden — the hidden being that a rival contend with you for the heart: a passion, a share, an attachment that draws you from Him. The parable of the fall depicts the collapse of one who severs himself from the One.

mots.racine: ح ن ف
خ ط ف / ط ي ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ وَمَن يُعَظِّمْ شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّهَا مِن تَقْوَى الْقُلُوبِ
versets.tr: dhālika wa-man yuʿaẓẓim shaʿāʾira llāhi fa-innahā min taqwā l-qulūbi
versets.trad: Such it is. And whoever honors the rites of God — that comes from the godfearing of hearts.
versets.commentaire: The believer recognizes God's rites by the manifest witness of knowledge, and by the secret inspirations of the heart. As one cannot contradict the witness of the Law, says Qushayrī, one cannot contradict the witness of the Real's truthful inspirations, for the Real's inspiration does not lie — rare is the one who can abide by it. As the soul does not speak truth, the heart does not lie; and whoever contradicts his heart, it goes blind for the future and the unveilings of Reality are cut off from him. The heart is strengthened by the realization of the divine descent.

mots.racine: ش ع ر
و ق ي / ق ل ب

versets.ar: لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَافِعُ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ثُمَّ مَحِلُّهَا إِلَى الْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيقِ
versets.tr: lakum fīhā manāfiʿu ilā ajalin musamman thumma maḥilluhā ilā l-bayti l-ʿatīqi
versets.trad: In them are benefits for you until an appointed term, then their place of sacrifice is at the ancient House.
versets.commentaire: “For you in them are benefits”: each one, says Qushayrī, draws from them a good according to his measure and rank — for some, blessings that ward off trials from themselves and their goods; for others, the delights of their expansion; for others still, the sweetness of their obediences; and for others, the intimacy of their breaths. The visible profit conceals a profit according to each one's spiritual station.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / أ ج ل
ح ل ل
mots.def: maḥill: place where the offering becomes lawful.

versets.ar: وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ جَعَلْنَا مَنسَكًا لِّيَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَىٰ مَا رَزَقَهُم مِّن بَهِيمَةِ الْأَنْعَامِ ۗ فَإِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ فَلَهُ أَسْلِمُوا ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُخْبِتِينَ
versets.tr: wa-li-kulli ummatin jaʿalnā mansakan li-yadhkurū sma llāhi ʿalā mā razaqahum min bahīmati l-anʿāmi fa-ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun fa-lahu aslimū wa-bashshiri l-mukhbitīna
versets.trad: For every community We have appointed a rite, that they may mention the name of God over the grazing beasts He has provided them. Your God is one God, so submit to Him. And give glad tidings to the humble,
versets.commentaire: The Laws, says Qushayrī, differ in their dealings but agree in their knowledges. “Submit to Him”: this is to yield to His decree without frowning or inner repugnance. Islām here is taken in the sense of ikhlāṣ — sincerity: purifying the works of their flaws, then the character of its troubles, then the states, then the breaths. And the “humble” (mukhbitīn) are known by perseverance in obedience with uprightness, by perfect lowliness in constant reverence, through the bowing of the inmost self.

mots.racine: ن س ك
س ل م
خ ب ت
mots.def: mukhbit: who is at peace in lowliness before God.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ اللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ عَلَىٰ مَا أَصَابَهُمْ وَالْمُقِيمِي الصَّلَاةِ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna idhā dhukira llāhu wajilat qulūbuhum wa-ṣ-ṣābirīna ʿalā mā aṣābahum wa-l-muqīmī ṣ-ṣalāti wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūna
versets.trad: those whose hearts tremble when God is mentioned, who endure with patience what befalls them, who establish the prayer, and who spend of what We have provided them.
versets.commentaire: The trembling (wajal), says Qushayrī, divides into fear of chastisement, of an evil end, of departing in heedlessness, or shame before God; but above all, it is in proportion to the unveiling of the Real to the heart — for hearts, at the moment of contemplation and theophany, are seized with trembling and awe. And he distinguishes: trembling with cause — fear of a shortfall; trembling without cause — pure awe (hayba). The nearest to God in heart are, in this respect, those who fear Him most. Patience, in turn, is to remain at peace under the flow of the decree, demanding no escape.

mots.racine: و ج ل / ق ل ب
mots.def: wajila: to quiver with reverential fear.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ص و ب

versets.ar: وَالْبُدْنَ جَعَلْنَاهَا لَكُم مِّن شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ لَكُمْ فِيهَا خَيْرٌ ۖ فَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا صَوَافَّ ۖ فَإِذَا وَجَبَتْ جُنُوبُهَا فَكُلُوا مِنْهَا وَأَطْعِمُوا الْقَانِعَ وَالْمُعْتَرَّ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ سَخَّرْنَاهَا لَكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-budna jaʿalnāhā lakum min shaʿāʾiri llāhi lakum fīhā khayrun fa-dhkurū sma llāhi ʿalayhā ṣawāffa fa-idhā wajabat junūbuhā fa-kulū minhā wa-aṭʿimū l-qāniʿa wa-l-muʿtarra kadhālika sakhkharnāhā lakum laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: The sacrificial camels — We have made them for you among the rites of God; in them is good for you. So mention the name of God over them as they stand in line. And when their flanks fall, eat of them and feed the content and the beggar. Thus have We subjected them to you, that you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates the goods of these beasts: riding, carrying, milk, their submission to man despite their strength, their docility before children, their patience with thirst and scant fodder, and the gentleness of their nature that is soothed by song — all signs that call for gratitude. And “the content” (al-qāniʿ) is one who has cast off the veil of shame and shown his need, while “the beggar” (al-muʿtarr) endures and conceals his poverty.

mots.racine: ب د ن
و ج ب / ج ن ب
ق ن ع / ع ر ر

versets.ar: لَن يَنَالَ اللَّهَ لُحُومُهَا وَلَا دِمَاؤُهَا وَلَٰكِن يَنَالُهُ التَّقْوَىٰ مِنكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ سَخَّرَهَا لَكُمْ لِتُكَبِّرُوا اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا هَدَاكُمْ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: lan yanāla llāha luḥūmuhā wa-lā dimāʾuhā wa-lākin yanāluhu t-taqwā minkum kadhālika sakhkharahā lakum li-tukabbirū llāha ʿalā mā hadākum wa-bashshiri l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches God, but the godfearing devotion from you reaches Him. Thus has He subjected them to you, that you may magnify God for having guided you. And give glad tidings to those who do good.
versets.commentaire: The cardinal verse of the sūra. Qushayrī raises it to a principle: no worth lies in the materiality of acts — bodily, monetary, or mixed; the worth lies in their conjunction with sincerity (ikhlāṣ). When to the works of the limbs is joined the purifying of intention, freed from the gaze cast upon others, they become fit for acceptance. He specifies: taqwā is to contemplate the Real under the seal of oneness, without mingling your nearness with the gaze upon anyone, without taking from man any recompense for a deed. And iḥsān, by the ḥadīth, is “to worship God as though you saw Him” — its mark being the falling away of every heaviness from the heart.

mots.racine: ن ي ل / ل ح م
mots.sens: their flesh will not reach God

mots.racine: ن ي ل / و ق ي
ح س ن
mots.def: muḥsin: who worships as though seeing God.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدَافِعُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ خَوَّانٍ كَفُورٍ
versets.tr: inna llāha yudāfiʿu ʿani lladhīna āmanū inna llāha lā yuḥibbu kulla khawwānin kafūrin
versets.trad: Truly God defends those who believe. Truly God does not love any treacherous ingrate.
versets.commentaire: “God defends the believers”: Qushayrī hears it from within — He wards off from their breasts the assaults of the devil, from their hearts the whisperings of disobedience, from their spirits the surgings of forgetfulness. Then he unfolds the degrees of spiritual treachery: that of works through ostentation; that of states through the complacent gaze, vanity, and repose; the treachery of the renouncers who leave the world for compensation; that of the gnostics who incline toward a station or an honor; that of the lovers who seek an escape from the ardor or the lack.

mots.racine: د ف ع
خ و ن / ك ف ر
mots.def: khawwān: great traitor; kafūr: deeply ungrateful.

versets.ar: أُذِنَ لِلَّذِينَ يُقَاتَلُونَ بِأَنَّهُمْ ظُلِمُوا ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ نَصْرِهِمْ لَقَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: udhina lilladhīna yuqātalūna bi-annahum ẓulimū wa-inna llāha ʿalā naṣrihim la-qadīrun
versets.trad: Permission to fight is given to those who are fought against, because they have been wronged — and truly God has power to help them,
versets.commentaire: When harm or humiliation strikes the believers from enemies, Qushayrī recalls that the Real takes vengeance on their enemies for them: they themselves remain, most often, in surrender and stillness, while the decree runs to the extermination of whoever fights them. At times He establishes them in a position of victory and dominion; but in every case, the forms of the divine help come to pass, and God, all in all, prevails in His command.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن / ق ت ل
mots.sens: permission is given to those fought against

mots.racine: ظ ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِم بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ إِلَّا أَن يَقُولُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ اللَّهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لَّهُدِّمَتْ صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ يُذْكَرُ فِيهَا اسْمُ اللَّهِ كَثِيرًا ۗ وَلَيَنصُرَنَّ اللَّهُ مَن يَنصُرُهُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ
versets.tr: alladhīna ukhrijū min diyārihim bi-ghayri ḥaqqin illā an yaqūlū rabbunā llāhu wa-lawlā dafʿu llāhi n-nāsa baʿḍahum bi-baʿḍin la-huddimat ṣawāmiʿu wa-biyaʿun wa-ṣalawātun wa-masājidu yudhkaru fīhā smu llāhi kathīran wa-la-yanṣuranna llāhu man yanṣuruhu inna llāha la-qawiyyun ʿazīzun
versets.trad: those who were driven from their homes without right, only for saying: “Our Lord is God.” Were it not for God's repelling of some people by means of others, monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques in which the name of God is much mentioned would have been demolished. God will surely help those who help Him. Truly God is strong and mighty.
versets.commentaire: “The wronged is helped, even after a while; the reign of Truth prevails over that of falsehood,” says Qushayrī. He transposes this law to the heart: the soul and its assaults sometimes oppress the heart of those near to God; then the Real, with the armies of His turning, routs the base thoughts, and helps the army of realization with the reinforcements of unveilings. When the winds of solicitude blow upon these hearts, and the heat of forgetfulness disperses, God waters them with the dew of theophany, makes the flowers of expansion grow in them, and the day of union dawns there. God's repelling of some by others preserves the dwellings of worship.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / د و ر
د ف ع
mots.sens: God's repelling of some by others

mots.racine: ص م ع / ب ي ع

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ إِن مَّكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ وَأَمَرُوا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَنَهَوْا عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ عَاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ
versets.tr: alladhīna in makkannāhum fī l-arḍi aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātawu z-zakāta wa-amarū bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-nahaw ʿani l-munkari wa-lillāhi ʿāqibatu l-umūri
versets.trad: those who, if We give them power on earth, establish the prayer, give the alms, enjoin the right and forbid the wrong. And to God belongs the outcome of all affairs.
versets.commentaire: If their span is prolonged, says Qushayrī, they do not exhaust their works in drawing their shares nor in amassing what they covet of the world, but they fulfill Our rights. “To establish the prayer”: outwardly, and to maintain the inner connections; it is to know, before God, who you are, whom you confide in, who watches over you, who is near you. The alms of the rich is upon their goods; the alms of the poor is upon their states. And the enjoining of right begins with oneself: to guard the senses from transgression and to watch over the breaths, in reverence for His majesty.

mots.racine: م ك ن / أ ر ض
mots.sens: if We establish them on earth

mots.racine: ع ق ب / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَعَادٌ وَثَمُودُ
versets.tr: wa-in yukadhdhibūka fa-qad kadhdhabat qablahum qawmu nūḥin wa-ʿādun wa-thamūdu
versets.trad: If they deny you, the people of Noah, ʿĀd, and Thamūd denied before them,
versets.commentaire: These verses, says Qushayrī, are a consolation for the Prophet and a firm exhortation to patience in enduring the trials and ills his people inflicted on him. The denial the Messenger receives is nothing new: it falls within the long line of prophets called liars before him, whose lot was triumph and whose peoples' lot was ruin.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
ق و م / ع و د

versets.ar: وَقَوْمُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَقَوْمُ لُوطٍ
versets.tr: wa-qawmu ibrāhīma wa-qawmu lūṭin
versets.trad: and the people of Abraham, and the people of Lot,
versets.commentaire: The litany of denying peoples continues — the people of Abraham, the people of Lot. For Qushayrī, this recapitulation upholds the same lesson: the Messenger must endure as the prophets before him endured, for the rejection of Truth by people is a constant of the ages, and its end is always the deliverance of the messengers.

mots.racine: ق و م
ق و م

versets.ar: وَأَصْحَابُ مَدْيَنَ ۖ وَكُذِّبَ مُوسَىٰ فَأَمْلَيْتُ لِلْكَافِرِينَ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-aṣḥābu madyana wa-kudhdhiba mūsā fa-amlaytu lil-kāfirīna thumma akhadhtuhum fa-kayfa kāna nakīri
versets.trad: and the people of Midian; and Moses was denied. I granted respite to the disbelievers, then I seized them: and how was My reproof!
versets.commentaire: “I granted respite, then I seized them”: the reprieve is no oversight. Qushayrī recalls elsewhere that God leaves the wrongdoer in his wrongdoing for a time, loosens the rope for him, prolongs the respite — he then imagines he has escaped the grip of the decree; then He seizes him from where he does not expect. “How was My reproof!” seals the justice that manifests after patience.

mots.racine: ص ح ب
م ل و / ك ف ر
mots.sens: I granted respite to the disbelievers

mots.racine: ن ك ر

versets.ar: فَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَاهَا وَهِيَ ظَالِمَةٌ فَهِيَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَبِئْرٍ مُّعَطَّلَةٍ وَقَصْرٍ مَّشِيدٍ
versets.tr: fa-kaʾayyin min qaryatin ahlaknāhā wa-hiya ẓālimatun fa-hiya khāwiyatun ʿalā ʿurūshihā wa-biʾrin muʿaṭṭalatin wa-qaṣrin mashīdin
versets.trad: How many a town have We destroyed while it was doing wrong! Now it lies fallen on its roofs; and how many an abandoned well and a lofty palace!
versets.commentaire: Wrongdoing first ruins the dwelling of the heart. Qushayrī reads these ruins within: wrongdoing first ravages the homeland of the wrongdoer's rest, which is his heart — the desolation that overwhelms him comes from the narrowness of his breast and the rancor of the oppressed. The “abandoned well” figures the gushing springs within them, dried up when wrongdoing settled in; and the “lofty palace,” their secrets deserted by awe and intimacy, their spirits emptied of the lights of love.

mots.racine: خ و ي / ع ر ش
ب أ ر / ع ط ل / ق ص ر
mots.def: muʿaṭṭala: out of use; mashīd: built high.

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَتَكُونَ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ يَعْقِلُونَ بِهَا أَوْ آذَانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَا ۖ فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصَارُ وَلَٰكِن تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ الَّتِي فِي الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: a-fa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-takūna lahum qulūbun yaʿqilūna bihā aw ādhānun yasmaʿūna bihā fa-innahā lā taʿmā l-abṣāru wa-lākin taʿmā l-qulūbu llatī fī ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: Have they not journeyed through the land, that they might have hearts to understand or ears to hear? For it is not the eyes that are blind, but blind are the hearts within the breasts.
versets.commentaire: A central verse on the knowledge of the heart. Qushayrī: they had hearts by creation, but when their praiseworthy qualities left them, it was as though they had never had any. And since the blindness is that of the heart, if the heart can be described with sight and hearing, it can be described with all the faculties of the living: as the hearts see by the light of certainty, they perceive the breeze of God's turning by the scent of the secret — like Jacob saying “I sense the scent of Joseph,” a perception of inner realities, not of outward fragrances.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ع ق ل
ع م ي / ق ل ب / ص د ر
mots.sens: the hearts within the breasts go blind

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذَابِ وَلَن يُخْلِفَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ ۚ وَإِنَّ يَوْمًا عِندَ رَبِّكَ كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ
versets.tr: wa-yastaʿjilūnaka bi-l-ʿadhābi wa-lan yukhlifa llāhu waʿdahu wa-inna yawman ʿinda rabbika ka-alfi sanatin mimmā taʿuddūna
versets.trad: They press you to hasten the chastisement; but God will not fail His promise. And a single day with your Lord is like a thousand years of your reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Their disbelief, says Qushayrī, drives them to demand what He threatens them with: had they believed, they would have believed it true; and had they believed it true, they would have been at peace. On “a day like a thousand years,” he has this metaphysical stroke: days, with Him, are equal — He has no haste; a day and a thousand years are equal to Him. For He upon whom time does not run, but who makes time run, finds the presence and absence of time, its little and its much, alike.

mots.racine: ع ج ل
mots.sens: they press you for the chastisement

mots.racine: أ ل ف / س ن و

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَمْلَيْتُ لَهَا وَهِيَ ظَالِمَةٌ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهَا وَإِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-kaʾayyin min qaryatin amlaytu lahā wa-hiya ẓālimatun thumma akhadhtuhā wa-ilayya l-maṣīru
versets.trad: How many a town did I grant respite while it was doing wrong! Then I seized it. And to Me is the return!
versets.commentaire: The respite (imhāl) comes from God, Qushayrī explains: He leaves the wrongdoer in his wrongdoing for a time, loosens the rope and prolongs the reprieve, so that he imagines he has escaped the grip of the decree — that is the conjecture he wanted; then He seizes him from where he did not watch, and regret covers him, but its hour has passed. And he adds this sharp point: how could one keep by guile what the decree has consigned to vanish?

mots.racine: م ل و
ص ي ر

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّمَا أَنَا لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: qul yā ayyuhā n-nāsu innamā anā lakum nadhīrun mubīnun
versets.trad: Say: “O mankind! I am only a clear warner to you.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ascribes to the Prophet this saying: “I resemble you in form, but I differ from you in the inmost self; I am for whoever among you does good a bearer of glad tidings, and for whoever does evil a warner. And I have supported, by the establishing of proofs, what I have brought you of the command to obedience and beneficence.” Warning is no idle threat, but a light offered.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lahum maghfiratun wa-rizqun karīmun
versets.trad: Those who believe and do righteous deeds shall have forgiveness and a generous provision.
versets.commentaire: Forgiveness (maghfira), says Qushayrī, has its kinds: for one He veils his fault; for another He veils his good deeds to protect him from the complacent gaze; for another He veils his state lest renown become a trial for him; for yet another He veils him among His intimates. As for the “generous provision”: that which comes from the lawful, without toil, without any creature claiming its merit, and which one is moved to spend on the way of nearness.

mots.racine: غ ف ر
mots.def: maghfira: pardon, from the sense of veiling.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ك ر م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ سَعَوْا فِي آيَاتِنَا مُعَاجِزِينَ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna saʿaw fī āyātinā muʿājizīna ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: But those who strive against Our signs, seeking to thwart them — those are the companions of Hellfire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shows that their chastisement begins already in this world: in the immediate, desolation, the closing of the doors of guidance, the bitterness of life, and the trial of keeping company with merciless people who do not fear God; then in the hereafter, what they will suffer of painful chastisement in measure of their crimes. Whoever fights the signs of God first condemns himself.

mots.racine: س ع ي / أ ي ي
ع ج ز

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ وَلَا نَبِيٍّ إِلَّا إِذَا تَمَنَّىٰ أَلْقَى الشَّيْطَانُ فِي أُمْنِيَّتِهِ فَيَنسَخُ اللَّهُ مَا يُلْقِي الشَّيْطَانُ ثُمَّ يُحْكِمُ اللَّهُ آيَاتِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā min qablika min rasūlin wa-lā nabiyyin illā idhā tamannā alqā sh-shayṭānu fī umniyyatihi fa-yansakhu llāhu mā yulqi sh-shayṭānu thumma yuḥkimu llāhu āyātihi wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: We never sent before you any messenger or prophet but that, when he recited, Satan cast into his recitation; yet God abolishes what Satan casts, then God makes firm His signs. And God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī rules out any breach of prophetic infallibility: the devils assail the prophets, but they have over their states neither hold nor effect; and our Prophet is the most eminent of all. What comes from the devil is mere fancy and lure, with no part of real misguidance for the Prophet; and God at once abolishes what Satan casts, then makes firm His signs. Only those without any realization are deceived by it.

mots.racine: م ن ي / ل ق ي
mots.def: tamannā: to desire, recite; alqā: to cast.

mots.racine: ح ك م

versets.ar: لِّيَجْعَلَ مَا يُلْقِي الشَّيْطَانُ فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَالْقَاسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَفِي شِقَاقٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: li-yajʿala mā yulqi sh-shayṭānu fitnatan lilladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun wa-l-qāsiyati qulūbuhum wa-inna ẓ-ẓālimīna la-fī shiqāqin baʿīdin
versets.trad: that He may make what Satan casts a trial for those in whose hearts is a sickness and whose hearts are hardened. Truly the wrongdoers are in a far-reaching schism.
versets.commentaire: What Satan casts becomes a discriminating trial: a temptation for sick and hardened hearts, but with no effect on the protected. Qushayrī: as for those whom God has supported with the strength of preservation (ʿiṣma) and whom His solicitude has seized, they saw clearly and nothing harmed them. The trial separates the sound heart from the afflicted, and reveals the “far-reaching schism” of the wrongdoers.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / م ر ض
mots.def: maraḍ: sickness, doubt of the heart.

mots.racine: ش ق ق / ب ع د

versets.ar: وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَيُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ فَتُخْبِتَ لَهُ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَهَادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-li-yaʿlama lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma annahu l-ḥaqqu min rabbika fa-yuʾminū bihi fa-tukhbita lahu qulūbuhum wa-inna llāha la-hādi lladhīna āmanū ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: and that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the Truth from your Lord, and so believe in it, and their hearts humble themselves before it. And truly God guides those who believe to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: When God wills good for a servant, says Qushayrī, He supports him with the light of realization and aids him with beautiful preservation; he then discerns, by the beauty of his insight, the true from the false: no cloud of doubt shadows him, the veil of heedlessness lifts from him — as the morning mist has no effect on the sun's ray at full day. And the hearts that humble themselves (tukhbit) are those the Real has guided to the straight path.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
خ ب ت
mots.def: akhbata: to grow calm in humility.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَزَالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِّنْهُ حَتَّىٰ تَأْتِيَهُمُ السَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-lā yazālu lladhīna kafarū fī miryatin minhu ḥattā taʾtiyahumu s-sāʿatu baghtatan aw yaʾtiyahum ʿadhābu yawmin ʿaqīmin
versets.trad: And those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt of it until the Hour comes upon them suddenly, or the chastisement of a barren Day comes upon them.
versets.commentaire: The disbelievers remain in doubt (mirya) until the Hour surprises them or the chastisement of a “barren Day” — without morrow or escape — reaches them. Unlike the believer whom the light of certainty makes firm, the disbeliever shuts himself in suspicion; and the decree seizes him, by the principle Qushayrī recalls, from where he did not watch.

mots.racine: م ر ي
ع ق م

versets.ar: الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلَّهِ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ
versets.tr: al-mulku yawmaʾidhin lillāhi yaḥkumu baynahum fa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fī jannāti n-naʿīmi
versets.trad: The Kingdom on that Day belongs to God; He judges between them. So those who believe and do righteous deeds are in the Gardens of Delight,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī clarifies that God's kingship is not confined to one day: His reign is specified by no day, no moment delimits Him, no measure bounds His majesty; but on that Day the claims are broken, the conjectures rise away, the suppositions vanish. Then for the believers and the people of accord are delights, and for the disbelievers and the people of discord, chastisements.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: the Kingdom that Day to God

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن ع م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا فَأُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā fa-ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: while those who disbelieve and deny Our signs — for them is a humiliating chastisement.
versets.commentaire: For those who have disbelieved and denied the signs: a humiliating chastisement. Qushayrī sets the two destinies against each other in a terse phrase: for these a humiliating chastisement, for those a manifest grace. The ignominy (muhīn) of the chastisement is the reverse of the honor (karāma) reserved for the faithful.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ي ي
ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلُوا أَوْ مَاتُوا لَيَرْزُقَنَّهُمُ اللَّهُ رِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna hājarū fī sabīli llāhi thumma qutilū aw mātū la-yarzuqannahumu llāhu rizqan ḥasanan wa-inna llāha la-huwa khayru r-rāziqīna
versets.trad: Those who emigrated in the way of God and were then killed or died, God will surely provide them with a good provision. Truly God is the best of providers.
versets.commentaire: To those who emigrated for God, Qushayrī reserves a threefold inner provision: for the hearts the sweetness of gnosis, for the spirits the robe of love, for the secrets the permanence of contemplation. Emigration (hijra) is not merely the displacement of bodies, but a going-out from all that is not God toward the good provision He dispenses, He the best of providers.

mots.racine: ه ج ر / س ب ل
mots.sens: they emigrated in the way of God
mots.def: hājara: to emigrate, to break away.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ر ز ق

versets.ar: لَيُدْخِلَنَّهُم مُّدْخَلًا يَرْضَوْنَهُ ۗ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَلِيمٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: la-yudkhilannahum mudkhalan yarḍawnahu wa-inna llāha la-ʿalīmun ḥalīmun
versets.trad: He will surely admit them to an entrance that pleases them. Truly God is all-knowing, most forbearing.
versets.commentaire: “An entrance that pleases them”: and Qushayrī wants it beyond their expectation. An entrance higher than what they wished, and a preserving of the state they hoped for — this at the moment of their lucidity, that they may grasp the subtleties of intimacy in their perfection and reach the delights of expansion at the highest of the states of joy. The Real gives more than the longing longs for.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ر ض و
mots.def: mudkhal: place of entry; raḍiya: to be pleased.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ل م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ وَمَنْ عَاقَبَ بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبَ بِهِ ثُمَّ بُغِيَ عَلَيْهِ لَيَنصُرَنَّهُ اللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ
versets.tr: dhālika wa-man ʿāqaba bi-mithli mā ʿūqiba bihi thumma bughiya ʿalayhi la-yanṣurannahu llāhu inna llāha la-ʿafuwwun ghafūrun
versets.trad: Such it is. And whoever retaliates with the like of what was done to him, and is then wronged again, God will surely help him. Truly God is pardoning, forgiving.
versets.commentaire: “God will surely help him”: Qushayrī describes this help of the friends as a mighty help, a complete vengeance, a total annihilation of the enemies, such that the helped one needs neither stratagem nor support from his fellows. The divine help suffices, and dispenses with every human contrivance: God alone is the Victor, and He is the Effacer, the Forgiver.

mots.racine: ع ق ب
mots.sens: he retaliates with the like of the offense

mots.racine: ع ف و / غ ف ر
mots.def: ʿafuww: who effaces; ghafūr: most forgiving.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha yūliju l-layla fī n-nahāri wa-yūliju n-nahāra fī l-layli wa-anna llāha samīʿun baṣīrun
versets.trad: That is because God merges the night into the day and merges the day into the night, and because God is all-hearing, all-seeing.
themes: the alternation of day and night
night and day of the secret
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the cosmic alternation to the inner world: as there is in the world's horizon a night and a day, there is one for the secrets: at theophany the day, at veiling the night. By the measure of seizure (qabḍ), it is night; by the measure of expansion (basṭ), it is day, and one grows over the other. This is for the gnostics; as for the realized, they have intimacy and awe in place of seizure and expansion — and some have a permanent day into which no night enters: this is the mark of the people of intimacy.

mots.racine: و ل ج / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: He merges the night into the day

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.def: samīʿ: who hears; baṣīr: who sees.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ وَأَنَّ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ هُوَ الْبَاطِلُ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha huwa l-ḥaqqu wa-anna mā yadʿūna min dūnihi huwa l-bāṭilu wa-anna llāha huwa l-ʿaliyyu l-kabīru
versets.trad: That is because God is the Truth, and what they call upon besides Him is falsehood, and because God is the Most High, the Most Great.
versets.commentaire: “God is the Real, and what they call upon besides Him is the vain.” Qushayrī reads here the march of contemplative annihilation: when a knowledge of the realities dawns, a fragment of extinction (fanāʾ) comes upon the one who receives the theophany; then the appearance of what shows itself grows and prevails, the traces of dispersion decrease and are effaced. And at the term: when the servant wholly withdraws from sensing what is other than God, he no longer contemplates things except for the Real, then by the Real, then he contemplates none but the Real — forgetting even himself and the whole universe.

mots.racine: ب ط ل
ع ل و / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the Most High, the Most Great

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَتُصْبِحُ الْأَرْضُ مُخْضَرَّةً ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-tuṣbiḥu l-arḍu mukhḍarratan inna llāha laṭīfun khabīrun
versets.trad: Do you not see that God sends down water from the sky, and the earth becomes green by morning? Truly God is subtle, all-aware.
versets.commentaire: From the water of the sky that revives the dead earth, Qushayrī draws a cascade of spiritual images: the water of mercy revives the states of the faulty after they have left their fault; the water of solicitude revives the states of people after the fading of their luster; the water of union revives the people of nearness after their aridity. Every outward greening figures an inner vivification by grace.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س م و / م و ه
mots.sens: He sends down water from the sky

mots.racine: ل ط ف / خ ب ر
mots.def: laṭīf: subtly benevolent; khabīr: who knows deeply.

versets.ar: لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-inna llāha la-huwa l-ghaniyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Truly God is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: All belongs to Him, and of all He is free: He is not enriched by His kingship; rather, His kingship exists only by His creating of things. On “the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy,” Qushayrī meditates: if the Rich is called Praised, it is that He is worthy of praise — whether He gives or withholds: if He gives, He merits the praise that is gratitude; if He withholds, He merits the praise that is pure eulogy. Hence, he says, our “Praise be to God, Lord of the worlds”: we thank Him in joy and praise Him in trial.

mots.racine: س م و
mots.sens: to Him what is in the heavens

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَالْفُلْكَ تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِ وَيُمْسِكُ السَّمَاءَ أَن تَقَعَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِالنَّاسِ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha sakhkhara lakum mā fī l-arḍi wa-l-fulka tajrī fī l-baḥri bi-amrihi wa-yumsiku s-samāʾa an taqaʿa ʿalā l-arḍi illā bi-idhnihi inna llāha bi-n-nāsi la-raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Do you not see that God has subjected to you what is on the earth, and the ships that run upon the sea by His command? He holds back the sky from falling upon the earth, except by His leave. Truly God is to mankind most kind, most merciful.
versets.commentaire: The subjection, says Qushayrī, is a subjection of enjoyment: what man profits from and is made easy for him is as if placed at his service. But he draws a decisive distinction: whoever enjoys a thing by way of God's permission, call, and command, it is favor and honor; whoever enjoys it contrariwise, it is ruse and gradual lure (istidrāj). The ship, the inspiration to build and use it, count among the greatest of God's blessings and His gentleness toward the servant.

mots.racine: س خ ر
mots.sens: He subjected to you what is on earth
mots.def: sakhkhara: to subject, set at service.

mots.racine: م س ك
ر أ ف / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَحْيَاكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَكَفُورٌ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī aḥyākum thumma yumītukum thumma yuḥyīkum inna l-insāna la-kafūrun
versets.trad: It is He who gave you life, then causes you to die, then will give you life again. Truly man is ungrateful.
versets.commentaire: The life and death of souls are of a limited number, says Qushayrī, but the life and death of the moments of the worshippers are without number or measure — after the verse: “I die when I remember You, then I live; how often for You I live and die!” He adds: this is the state of the common; as for the elect, their life is perpetual and their reviving eternal. Indeed, how should he die for whom the existence of the Real supplies all that absents itself?

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gave you life then makes you die
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life; amāta: to make die.

mots.racine: ك ف ر

versets.ar: لِّكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ جَعَلْنَا مَنسَكًا هُمْ نَاسِكُوهُ ۖ فَلَا يُنَازِعُنَّكَ فِي الْأَمْرِ ۚ وَادْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ هُدًى مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: li-kulli ummatin jaʿalnā mansakan hum nāsikūhu fa-lā yunāziʿunnaka fī l-amri wa-dʿu ilā rabbika innaka la-ʿalā hudan mustaqīmin
versets.trad: For every community We have appointed a rite which they observe. So let them not dispute with you in the matter. And call to your Lord; truly you are upon a straight guidance.
versets.commentaire: God, says Qushayrī, has given each group a Law it follows, each community a way it travels; He has assigned to each station its dwellers, to each abode its residents. And he draws a geography of the hearts: the carpet of worship is trodden by the feet of the worshippers, the places of striving are peopled by the men of effort, the assemblies of the gnostics are inhabited by the presence of the gnostics, and the dwellings of the lovers by the ecstatics.

mots.racine: ن س ك
mots.def: mansak: rite; nāsik: who performs it.

mots.racine: د ع و

versets.ar: وَإِن جَادَلُوكَ فَقُلِ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in jādalūka fa-quli llāhu aʿlamu bimā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: And if they dispute with you, say: “God knows best what you do.”
versets.commentaire: “Entrust them all to Us,” Qushayrī has it said, when they seek dispute; do not rely on the stratagems you might choose, and beware of inclining, in your heart, toward seeking support in peers and likes: for they are empty molds, phantoms void of all meaning. The Prophet is referred to the sole support of God, who knows what they do.

mots.racine: ج د ل
ع ل م / ع م ل
mots.sens: God knows best what you do

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu yaḥkumu baynakum yawma l-qiyāmati fīmā kuntum fīhi takhtalifūna
versets.trad: God will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein you used to differ.
versets.commentaire: On the Day of Resurrection, God will decide your differences. Qushayrī distinguishes the lots: to the strangers He will say, “your own soul suffices this day to call you to account”; some of the near He will judge with a light reckoning; and to certain singular elect He will say: “between Me and you is a reckoning” — no Gabriel judges between them, no Michael, no sent prophet, no angel brought near. He alone judges, and settles between the parties.

mots.racine: ح ك م
خ ل ف

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ فِي كِتَابٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-lam taʿlam anna llāha yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi inna dhālika fī kitābin inna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīrun
versets.trad: Do you not know that God knows what is in the heaven and the earth? That is in a Book; that is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: God knows the secret and the whispered. Qushayrī makes of this knowledge a tenderness: He knows the secret and the murmur, and the servant's need is nearer still to Him and more present; in every way, He is more entitled to the servant than the servant is to himself. His it is to bear the blessing, to remove the trial, hearing nothing of the complaint: His is the decree, blessed and exalted. All is easy for Him from whom nothing escapes the Book.

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.sens: He knows what is in heaven and earth

mots.racine: ي س ر

versets.ar: وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطَانًا وَمَا لَيْسَ لَهُم بِهِ عِلْمٌ ۗ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-yaʿbudūna min dūni llāhi mā lam yunazzil bihi sulṭānan wa-mā laysa lahum bihi ʿilmun wa-mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min naṣīrin
versets.trad: They worship besides God that for which He has sent down no authority, and of which they have no knowledge. And the wrongdoers shall have no helper.
versets.commentaire: The verse, Qushayrī reads, indicates by contrast the lot of the elect: whoever is among His intimates, God singles him out with a proof (burhān), supports him with an evidence (bayān), strengthens him with an authority (sulṭān). Conversely, whoever has no authority, His dominion extends over him; whoever has no proof, his light turns away toward another — and there he is, held apart from His nearness.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س ل ط
mots.sens: that for which no authority is sent down

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ تَعْرِفُ فِي وُجُوهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْمُنكَرَ ۖ يَكَادُونَ يَسْطُونَ بِالَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِنَا ۗ قُلْ أَفَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمُ ۗ النَّارُ وَعَدَهَا اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin taʿrifu fī wujūhi lladhīna kafarū l-munkara yakādūna yasṭūna bi-lladhīna yatlūna ʿalayhim āyātinā qul a-fa-unabbiʾukum bi-sharrin min dhālikum an-nāru waʿadahā llāhu lladhīna kafarū wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: When Our clear signs are recited to them, you recognize aversion on the faces of those who disbelieve. They almost assail those who recite Our signs to them. Say: “Shall I tell you of something worse than that? The Fire, which God has promised to those who disbelieve, and what an evil destination!”
versets.commentaire: The hearing of the divine discourse, says Qushayrī, leaves a trace in the hearts: blossoming and joy, or aversion and desolation; then what the inmost self holds surfaces on the face. When the verses came down and were recited to the disbelievers, there showed on their faces the smoke of what their hearts concealed of the darknesses of denial — no gaze fell upon them without revealing their refusal. The face is the mirror of the within.

mots.racine: ع ر ف / و ج ه / ن ك ر
mots.sens: you recognize aversion on their faces

mots.racine: ك و د / س ط و

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ لَن يَخْلُقُوا ذُبَابًا وَلَوِ اجْتَمَعُوا لَهُ ۖ وَإِن يَسْلُبْهُمُ الذُّبَابُ شَيْئًا لَّا يَسْتَنقِذُوهُ مِنْهُ ۚ ضَعُفَ الطَّالِبُ وَالْمَطْلُوبُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu ḍuriba mathalun fa-stamiʿū lahu inna lladhīna tadʿūna min dūni llāhi lan yakhluqū dhubāban wa-lawi jtamaʿū lahu wa-in yaslubhumu dh-dhubābu shayʾan lā yastanqidhūhu minhu ḍaʿufa ṭ-ṭālibu wa-l-maṭlūbu
versets.trad: O mankind! A parable is set forth, so listen to it! Those whom you call upon besides God will never create a fly, even if they all gathered for it; and if the fly should snatch something from them, they could not retrieve it from it. Feeble are the seeker and the sought!
versets.commentaire: God rouses “the scattered thoughts and the dispersed minds,” says Qushayrī, to gather them to listen: “a parable is set forth, listen to it.” Those they invoke and name gods could not, all gathered, create a fly, nor anything less; and if the fly snatches something from them, they have no power to retrieve it. And he concludes, cutting: whoever cannot stand against a fly and is overcome by it — how despicable is his rank!

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل / س م ع
mots.sens: a parable is set forth, listen to it
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to set an example.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ذ ب ب
mots.sens: they will never create a fly

mots.racine: ض ع ف / ط ل ب

versets.ar: مَا قَدَرُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ
versets.tr: mā qadarū llāha ḥaqqa qadrihi inna llāha la-qawiyyun ʿazīzun
versets.trad: They did not measure God His true measure. Truly God is strong, mighty!
versets.commentaire: “They did not measure God His true measure,” that is, says Qushayrī, they did not know Him with His true knowledge, nor describe Him with the majesty of the attributes He merits. He distinguishes two efforts: that of the common, who reject impure works out of fear of God; and that of the elect, who exhaust themselves stripping their belief of the attributes the Pure-Sufficiency (ṣamadiyya) is too exalted for — and between the two, an abyss. “ʿAzīz” also means: none measures His true measure, save within the limit of human knowledge.

mots.racine: ق د ر
mots.sens: they did not measure God His measure

mots.racine: ق و ي / ع ز ز

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَصْطَفِي مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ رُسُلًا وَمِنَ النَّاسِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: allāhu yaṣṭafī mina l-malāʾikati rusulan wa-mina n-nāsi inna llāha samīʿun baṣīrun
versets.trad: God chooses messengers from among the angels and from among mankind. Truly God is all-hearing, all-seeing.
versets.commentaire: Election (iṣṭifāʾ), says Qushayrī, comes from the Real by the establishing of rank, the singling-out of grace, and their precedence over their peers in merits and gifts. Then they are “some above others in degrees”: excellence belongs to the right of the messenger as messenger, not to some particularity in the nature of the messenger. Election is pure gift, not acquired merit.

mots.racine: ص ف و / م ل ك / ر س ل
mots.sens: He chooses messengers from the angels

mots.racine: ن و س

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۗ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: yaʿlamu mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūru
versets.trad: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and to God all affairs are returned.
versets.commentaire: God, says Qushayrī, knows their present state and their becoming, their outward and their secret, their today and their tomorrow; He even knows their breaking of the pact — for to Him is their return, and in His grip is their turning. Nothing of the “before” or the “behind” escapes Him, and all comes to its end in Him.

mots.racine: ي د ي / خ ل ف
mots.sens: what is before and behind them

mots.racine: ر ج ع / أ م ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا وَاعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَافْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū rkaʿū wa-sjudū wa-ʿbudū rabbakum wa-fʿalū l-khayra laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Bow down, prostrate yourselves, worship your Lord, and do good, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Bowing, prostration, worship: all this, says Qushayrī, denotes the prayer. But God apportioned its words out of regard for your heart seized with fear at the command to pray — He fragmented it so that with each word and each meaning an easing might attach; and for the hearts of the gnostics, in each word is a fresh repose. More still: He knows that the beloved love to hear His speech, so He lengthened the discourse for them — that in hearing it intimacy might grow upon intimacy, spirit upon spirit; for the return of the Beloved's address is the spirit of their spirit and the fullness of their repose.

mots.racine: ر ك ع / س ج د
mots.def: rakaʿa: to bow; sajada: to prostrate.

mots.racine: ف ل ح

versets.ar: وَجَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ ۚ هُوَ اجْتَبَاكُمْ وَمَا جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ ۚ مِّلَّةَ أَبِيكُمْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ۚ هُوَ سَمَّاكُمُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ مِن قَبْلُ وَفِي هَٰذَا لِيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ شَهِيدًا عَلَيْكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا شُهَدَاءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ ۚ فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِاللَّهِ هُوَ مَوْلَاكُمْ ۖ فَنِعْمَ الْمَوْلَىٰ وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-jāhidū fī llāhi ḥaqqa jihādihi huwa jtabākum wa-mā jaʿala ʿalaykum fī d-dīni min ḥarajin millata abīkum ibrāhīma huwa sammākumu l-muslimīna min qablu wa-fī hādhā li-yakūna r-rasūlu shahīdan ʿalaykum wa-takūnū shuhadāʾa ʿalā n-nāsi fa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta wa-ʿtaṣimū bi-llāhi huwa mawlākum fa-niʿma l-mawlā wa-niʿma n-naṣīru
versets.trad: Strive for God a true striving. He has chosen you and laid upon you no hardship in religion — the faith of your father Abraham. He named you Muslims before, and in this, that the Messenger may be a witness over you and you witnesses over mankind. So establish the prayer, give the alms, and hold fast to God: He is your Protector — what an excellent Protector and what an excellent Helper!
versets.commentaire: The seal of the sūra. The “true striving” (ḥaqq jihādihi), says Qushayrī, is that which accords with the command in its measure, its time, and its kind, and he declines it: striving by the soul — to reserve no strength one does not spend in obedience, seeking no ease; striving by the heart — to preserve it from base thoughts and heedlessness; striving by good — giving, generosity, preferring others to oneself. On “He has chosen you”: had He not made His election of you, you would not have striven; it is by His election that He granted you to succeed in it. And “hold fast to God” (iʿtaṣimū): to disown all power and strength of one's own, to rise to the worship of God by God for God. He seals it on tenderness: “What an excellent Protector” tells His grandeur, “what an excellent Helper” tells His mercy; He loved you before you loved Him, knew you before you knew Him — and at the tomb, when all forsake you, He alone remains to you.

mots.racine: ج ه د
mots.sens: strive for God a true striving

mots.racine: م ل ل / أ ب و
mots.sens: the faith of your father Abraham

mots.racine: ع ص م
و ل ي / ن ص ر
mots.sens: what an excellent Protector and Helper
